rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative selenylation/cyclization of 4-arylpent-4-enoic acids with diaryl (or dialkyl) diselenides to afford selenated gamma-lactones in an undivided cell with Pt electrodes and LiClO4/MeCN.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of PhSeSePh to its radical cation, which fragments into PhSe+ and PhSe radical; the alkenoic acid is also oxidizable (Eox = 2.4 V) and a radical-addition intermediate is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation. Protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

key_intermediates.name: seleniranium ion (IV) from PhSe+ addition to alkene of 1a
beta-selenyl carbon radical (V) from PhSe• addition to alkene
beta-selenyl carbocation (VI) from anodic oxidation of V

electron_flow: Path a (ionic): anode oxidizes PhSeSePh to a radical cation that fragments to PhSe+ and PhSe•; PhSe+ forms a seleniranium with the alkene, and intramolecular carboxylate attack closes the gamma-lactone. Path b (radical): PhSe• adds to the alkene to give a C-radical, which is anodically oxidized to a carbocation, then trapped by the pendant carboxylate with deprotonation; protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of 3a decreased to 44% but reaction not fully inhibited, implicating a partial radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of 3a reduced to 67%; partial inhibition consistent with radical involvement.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethene
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of 3a reduced to 65%; partial inhibition consistent with radical involvement.

probe_reagents.name: cyclopropyl-substituted styrene (16)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened/rearranged product 17 obtained in 41% (E:Z = 1.3:1), supporting a radical addition pathway.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1a and 2a
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peaks at 2.4 V (1a) and 1.7 V (2a), indicating diselenide is preferentially oxidized at the anode.

probe_reagents.name: PhSSPh (disulfide 14) in place of diselenide
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace lactone 15 formed, showing the chemistry is specific to Se radical/cation chemistry.

notes: Authors explicitly propose two parallel pathways (path a, ionic via seleniranium from PhSe+; path b, radical via PhSe• addition then anodic oxidation to a cation). They state they cannot rule out the radical pathway; both converge on the same carbocation-like intermediate trapped by the carboxylate. Control with disulfide failed (only trace product), and substrate scope shows alkyl diselenides also work, supporting a general Se electrophile/radical mechanism.
